canonical_definition: The latent tendency of an LLM to produce reductive, exaggerated persona-based narratives by disproportionately amplifying identity-specific traits while maintaining recognizability.

definitions.text: The latent tendency of an LLM to produce reductive, exaggerated persona-based narratives by disproportionately amplifying identity-specific traits while maintaining recognizability.

relationships.evidence: Recent work formalizes this phenomenon [Caricature] through two measurable components: individuation—the extent to which a response diverges from a neutral baseline—and exaggeration—the degree to which persona-linked semantics dominate beyond topical necessity (Cheng et al., 2023). (Section 2.2)

characterization: The latent tendency of an LLM to produce reductive, exaggerated persona-based narratives by disproportionately amplifying identity-specific traits while maintaining recognizability.
